evidence_excerpt: He lived a normal life, and no neurologic abnormalities were found at the 6-month follow-up; imaging verified the disappearance of the left EICA aneurysm after ligation.

quality_of_life.functional_status_evidence_excerpt: Normal life with no neurologic abnormalities at 6-month follow-up after discharge.
quality_of_life.symptom_burden_evidence_excerpt: No neurologic abnormalities reported at follow-up; patient lived a normal life.

timeline_events.evidence_excerpt: Emergency CT showed hemorrhagic stroke in the right basal ganglia region 10 days after the left STA-MCA bypass.
Muscle strength in the left limbs deteriorated from grade 5 to grade 2 about 20 days following the operations.
After 20 days of medical treatment, the hematoma disappeared and muscle strength returned to grade 4.
Right STA-MCA bypass performed 6 months after the left bypass.
Patient was discharged with no neurologic abnormalities 10 days after the right bypass.
At 6-month follow-up after discharge, he lived a normal life with no neurologic abnormalities.

notes_on_uncertainty: Index time was approximated as the hospital admission when diagnosis and treatment planning occurred. Long-term clinical follow-up is reported at ~6 months after discharge (approximately ~12 months after admission), while imaging confirmation of aneurysm disappearance was noted at 12 months; functional status beyond that interval is not detailed.
